final: It triggers whenever a capability/provider is classified as cloud-backed — in practice, that means its host is not localhost, 127.0.0.1, or a private IP, so the request is treated as `is_cloud=True`. The user sees a consent prompt/gate before the capability is allowed to execute, so cloud calls don’t happen silently. The decision is owned by `emptyos/sdk/capabilities.py` in `Capability.execute()` (with the policy grounded by the cloud consent rule in `docs/DESIGN.md`).
